turns.discoveryQuestion: Are you evaluating Algolia for your own company, or on behalf of clients?

turns.userInput: We're a $2B online fashion retailer with 5 million SKUs and 80 million monthly visitors. Our current search is Elasticsearch.
turns.answer:
Explore with MaverickAlright, a $2B fashion house doing search on ES—I see you. You're at the scale where search relevance directly impacts your bottom line. You're not just ranking products; you're curating experiences. And with 80 million monthly visitors, even a tiny lift in conversion can mean millions in revenue.

The Algolia Edge: From Matching to Understanding
Traditional search, like Elasticsearch, relies on keyword matching. Algolia's AI goes further How to use AI to build your business. We understand the intent behind the query, not just the words. Think "something to hold my hot coffee" vs. "keep my coffee hot." This is the shift from matching to understanding, and it's where serious e-commerce brands are headed.
And our AI improves every aspect of the user journey Compare Algolia & Constructor, from automatic query categorization and re-ranking to personalization and recommendations. turns.userInput: I'm the VP of E-Commerce. We need better relevance — customers can't find products when they search naturally.
turns.answer:
Explore with MaverickI hear you loud and clear. "Can't find it" = "Can't buy it," and that's revenue walking out the door. You're the VP of E-Commerce; you're not just tweaking algorithms, you're building a discovery experience that converts.

From "Little Black Dress" to "Cocktail Attire"
The problem you're describing is exactly what Algolia solves for fashion retailers. Customers search like they think, not like a search engine expects. They type, "I need a statement piece for a wedding after-party," not just "red dress" Algolia Launches Intelligent Fashion Solution.
Algolia's AI understands that intent. We make sure that "little black dress" never hides under "cocktail attire," ensuring every product finds its audience effortlessly Algolia Launches Intelligent Fashion Solution.

Example: Oh Polly
Take Oh Polly, for instance. They were getting negative feedback because their old search was slow and didn't provide relevant results.
